summary: Design or refresh an onboarding process with ramp plans, checklists, and manager handoff checkpoints.

inputs.name: role_context
inputs.description: Role definition, team structure, and reporting lines.

inputs.name: ramp_expectations
inputs.description: Expected ramp timeline, milestones, and success criteria.

inputs.name: organizational_context
inputs.description: Company culture, tools, and compliance requirements.

outputs.name: onboarding_plan
outputs.description: Structured onboarding sequence with timelines and owners.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Preserve new hire experience and human decision ownership.

gates.rule: Confirm the request is onboarding process design, not legal advice or autonomous HR approval.
Require role context and ramp expectations before drafting onboarding plan.
Mark outputs as decision-support artifacts and include a human manager checkpoint.
Return clear onboarding sequence and note where policy or compliance decisions need human approval.

steps.action: Set onboarding objectives, cultural integration goals, and compliance requirements.
Create 30-60-90 day milestones with success criteria and owners.
Design manager checklist, check-in cadence, and feedback loops.
Merge onboarding plan, ramp plan, and manager handoff into one reviewer-ready pack.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read role profiles, team structures, and existing onboarding materials.

verification.method: Check the plan names ramp goals with milestones before onboarding steps.
Check each onboarding step has an owner or responsible team.
Check manager checklist and check-in cadence are included.

success_criteria: The onboarding plan can be executed without guessing ramp goals or step ownership.
Manager handoff is clear enough for consistent new hire experience.
The output clearly states that policy and compliance decisions remain with human managers.

rollback.when: If the plan omits ramp goals or manager handoff, revert to role context and rebuild.
